// CreateFile creates any file in the test environment
func (te *TestEnvironment) CreateFile(relativePath, content string) {
	te.t.Helper()
	fullPath := filepath.Join(te.Dir, relativePath)
	dir := filepath.Dir(fullPath)
	require.NoError(te.t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755))
	require.NoError(te.t, os.WriteFile(fullPath, []byte(content), 0644))
}

// NewTestRootCommand creates a fresh root command for tests using isolated temp directory
// This is the preferred way to create test commands - it ensures test isolation.
func NewTestRootCommand(t *testing.T) *CommandFactory {
	t.Helper()
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	return NewCommandFactory(tempDir)
}

// NewTestRootCommandWithDir creates a test command with a specific working directory
// Use this when your test needs to operate in a specific directory.
func NewTestRootCommandWithDir(dir string) *CommandFactory {
	return NewCommandFactory(dir)
}

// executeCommand is a helper to execute commands with captured output (legacy compatibility)
// New tests should use TestEnvironment.RunCommand() instead
func executeCommand(root *cobra.Command, args ...string) (output string, err error) {
	buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
	root.SetOut(buf)
	root.SetErr(buf)
	root.SetArgs(args)

	err = root.Execute()
	return buf.String(), err
}

data.text: I’ve got the implementation seam now. Before I edit, I’m checking whether Cobra’s built-in version handling is already in play anywhere in this repo so I don’t accidentally collide with an existing flag convention or template path.

I want to avoid guessing Cobra’s no-args fallback, because the root `--version` path only works cleanly if I know whether the command will still fall through to help after the pre-run hook. I’m checking Cobra’s execution code directly from the module cache now.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'cobra_pkg=$(go env GOPATH)/pkg/mod; rg -n "func '"\\(c \\*Command\\) Execute\\(|func \\(c \\*Command\\) execute\" \""'$cobra_pkg"/github.com/spf13/cobra* -g '"'*.go' && printf '\\n---PRIMARY MATCH---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' "'$(rg -l "func '"\\(c \\*Command\\) Execute\\(\" \""'$cobra_pkg"/github.com/spf13/cobra* -g '"'*.go' | head -n 1)"

data.text: I’ve chosen the lowest-risk route: enable Cobra’s built-in root version flag support, then make its template emit the same three lines as the existing `version` subcommand. That keeps `-v` untouched and avoids forcing the root command through the normal pre-run/update path.
